name: Pumpkin-Squash Bisque - Country Living Holidays
recipeIngredient: 2 13 3/4 or 14 1/2 oz cans chicken broth, all surface fat removed
1 cup Sliced fresh or frozen cauliflower
recipeInstructions:
1. If using pumpkins for serving bowls, preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Rinse the pumpkins; pat dry. Cut a wide circle around the stem of each pumpkin, making a lid. Remove the lid and scrape out seeds and stringy portions from the pumpkins. Place the pumpkins and their lids in a large shallow roasting or baking pan. Cover with aluminum foil.
2. Bake the pumpkins for 30 to 50 minutes, or until the flesh is fork-tender. Test the lids for doneness after 30 minutes; if tender, remove and set aside. If using a microwave, place 3 or 4 pumpkins with their lids inserted in microwave ovens. Cook for 15 to 20 minutes, rotating every 5 minutes, or until tender.
3. In a 4-quart saucepan, heat the broth and cauliflower to boiling over high heat. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer the fresh cauliflower until tender (about 10 minutes). If using frozen cauliflower, cook for about 5 minutes. With a slotted spoon, remove the cauliflower to a blender or food processor, leaving the broth in the saucepan.
4. Add the onion to the remaining broth in the saucepan; cover and heat to boiling. Cook until tender (about 5 minutes). In a separate cup, combine 1/2 cup Milk, flour, salt, and pepper until smooth. Stir the flour mixture into the onion and broth. Cook the mixture, or sauce, stirring constantly, until it thickens and boils. Remove from heat.
5. In the blender, puree the cauliflower with half of the sauce and the half-and-half. Pour the cauliflower soup into a small saucepan and keep warm over very low heat.
6. In the same blender (no need to wash), blend the remaining sauce until smooth; return to the saucepan; set aside. Add half the mashed pumpkin and 3/4 cup Milk to the blender; blend until smooth; pour into the saucepan with the sauce. Repeat to puree the remaining mashed pumpkin with the remaining milk. Heat the pumpkin mixture, or bisque, over low heat just until hot, stirring constantly. Do not allow it to boil.
7. To serve, ladle the pumpkin bisque into baked pumpkin "bowls" or serving bowls and place on serving plates. Drizzle cauliflower soup on top of each serving.
